items.q: What is CI/CD and how have you implemented it using Jenkins?
items.hint: Think about your DigitalOne pipeline setup end to end.

items.q: Explain the difference between Continuous Integration, Continuous Delivery, and Continuous Deployment.
What is a declarative vs scripted Jenkins pipeline? Which do you prefer and why?
items.hint: You have hands-on experience — explain with real examples.

items.q: How do you set up and manage Jenkins Nodes (master-agent architecture)?
items.hint: You have managed Jenkins Nodes — explain the setup and purpose.

items.q: How do you trigger a Jenkins pipeline automatically on a code push?
items.hint: Webhooks from Bitbucket to Jenkins.

items.q: How do you handle a failed deployment and roll back using your pipeline?
items.hint: Helm rollback command, using previous image tags.

items.q: What monitoring tools have you used to observe deployments? How do you use Splunk?
items.hint: Splunk log search, dashboards, alerts.

items.q: Explain Blue-Green deployment vs Canary deployment. Have you implemented either?
What is the difference between a build pipeline and a release pipeline?
What is Jenkins and why is it widely used in DevOps?
What is a Jenkinsfile and what are the two types of pipeline syntax?
items.hint: Declarative vs Scripted pipeline.

items.q: How do you configure a Jenkins master-agent architecture? What are the benefits?
items.hint: Explain agent types: SSH, JNLP.

items.q: How do you manage user roles and permissions in Jenkins?
items.hint: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) plugin.

items.q: How do you integrate Jenkins with Bitbucket for automated builds?
items.hint: Webhooks, Bitbucket plugin, branch strategies.

items.q: What is a shared library in Jenkins and why would you use it?
items.hint: Reuse pipeline code across multiple projects.

items.q: How do you pass parameters between stages in a Jenkins pipeline?
items.hint: Environment variables, stash/unstash.

items.q: How do you secure credentials in Jenkins pipelines?
items.hint: Jenkins credentials store, withCredentials block.

items.q: What are Jenkins build triggers? Explain SCM polling vs webhooks.
items.hint: Webhooks are preferred — real-time, less overhead.

items.q: How do you archive artifacts and publish test reports in Jenkins?
items.hint: archiveArtifacts, junit plugin.

items.q: How do you set up notifications in Jenkins on build failure?
items.hint: Post block in declarative pipeline — email or Slack.

items.q: How do you handle parallel stages in a Jenkins pipeline?
items.hint: parallel block in declarative pipeline.

items.q: What is the difference between a freestyle job and a pipeline job in Jenkins?
How do you troubleshoot a Jenkins build that is stuck or failing?
items.hint: Check executor, agent connectivity, logs, workspace.

items.q: How do you upgrade Jenkins and manage plugins safely?
items.hint: Test in staging, pin plugin versions, backup before upgrade.

items.q: What is Bitbucket and how does it differ from GitHub?
items.hint: Atlassian ecosystem, JIRA integration, self-hosted option.

items.q: What is a Bitbucket repository and how do you create one?
Explain the Git branching strategy you follow in your projects.
items.hint: Feature branch, develop, release, hotfix — GitFlow.

items.q: How do you create and review a Pull Request in Bitbucket?
items.hint: Code review, approvals, merge strategies.

items.q: What are Bitbucket Pipelines and how do they compare to Jenkins?
items.hint: Built-in CI/CD vs external Jenkins — tradeoffs.

items.q: How do you configure webhooks in Bitbucket to trigger Jenkins?
items.hint: Settings → Webhooks → Jenkins URL.

items.q: What is a Bitbucket branch permission and why do you use it?
items.hint: Protect main/release branches from direct pushes.

items.q: How do you resolve a merge conflict in Git/Bitbucket?
items.hint: git fetch, merge, resolve manually, commit.

items.q: What is the difference between git merge and git rebase?
items.hint: Merge preserves history, rebase rewrites it — cleaner log.

items.q: How do you tag a release in Bitbucket and why is it important?
items.hint: git tag — used for versioning and rollback reference.

items.q: How do you manage access control and permissions in Bitbucket?
items.hint: Project/repo level permissions, groups, SSH keys.

items.q: What is a Bitbucket fork and when would you use it?
items.hint: Independent copy for open-source or isolated development.

items.q: What is Ansible and how does it differ from Chef or Puppet?
items.hint: Agentless, YAML-based, push model.

items.q: What is an Ansible playbook? Walk me through its structure.
items.hint: hosts, vars, tasks, handlers sections.

items.q: What is an Ansible inventory file? What are static vs dynamic inventories?
items.hint: Static: INI/YAML file. Dynamic: script/plugin that queries cloud APIs.

items.q: What are Ansible roles and why do you use them?
items.hint: Reusable, modular playbook structure — roles folder layout.

items.q: What is the difference between Ansible variables, facts, and registers?
items.hint: vars: defined, facts: gathered, register: task output stored.

items.q: How do you handle sensitive data like passwords in Ansible?
items.hint: Ansible Vault — encrypt variables or entire files.

items.q: What are Ansible handlers and when are they triggered?
items.hint: Triggered only when notified by a task that changed.

items.q: How do you run an Ansible playbook in check mode (dry run)?
items.hint: --check flag — shows what would change without applying.

items.q: What is the difference between ansible ad-hoc commands and playbooks?
items.hint: Ad-hoc: one-off commands. Playbooks: repeatable automation.

items.q: How do you use Ansible to deploy an application across multiple servers?
items.hint: Inventory groups, rolling updates with serial keyword.

items.q: What are Ansible modules? Name some commonly used ones.
items.hint: yum, apt, copy, template, service, file, command, shell.

items.q: How do you use Ansible for configuration management in a DevOps pipeline?
items.hint: Integrate with Jenkins — run playbook after Terraform provisions infra.

items.q: What experience do you have with Windows Server administration?
items.hint: Windows 2003/2008/2012 R2/2022 experience.

items.q: What is IIS and what have you used it for?
items.hint: Web server for hosting .NET and banking apps on Windows.

items.q: How do you install and configure IIS on Windows Server?
items.hint: Server Manager → Add Roles → Web Server (IIS).

items.q: How do you deploy a web application to IIS?
items.hint: Application pool, site binding, virtual directory setup.

items.q: What is an Application Pool in IIS and why is it important?
items.hint: Isolation, worker process (w3wp.exe), identity, recycling settings.

items.q: How do you configure SSL/TLS certificates in IIS?
items.hint: Server Certificates → bind HTTPS → select certificate.

items.q: How do you troubleshoot a 500 Internal Server Error in IIS?
items.hint: Event Viewer, IIS logs (C:\\inetpub\\logs), Failed Request Tracing.

items.q: What are IIS log files and where are they located?
items.hint: C:\\inetpub\\logs\\LogFiles — W3C format, useful for troubleshooting.

items.q: How do you configure URL rewrite rules in IIS?
items.hint: URL Rewrite module — redirect HTTP to HTTPS, clean URLs.

items.q: What is Windows Server 2022 and what are its key improvements?
items.hint: Secured-core server, Azure Arc integration, SMB compression.

items.q: How do you manage Windows services and scheduled tasks?
items.hint: services.msc, Task Scheduler, sc.exe, PowerShell.

items.q: How do you monitor Windows Server performance?
items.hint: Task Manager, Resource Monitor, Performance Monitor, Event Viewer.

items.q: What is Active Directory and how have you used it?
items.hint: User/group management, GPO — directory services.

items.q: How do you patch and update Windows Server securely?
items.hint: WSUS, Windows Update, patch scheduling, maintenance windows.

items.q: What is Apache Tomcat and what is it used for?
items.hint: Java Servlet container — hosts Java web applications (WAR files).

items.q: What is the directory structure of Tomcat? Explain key folders.
items.hint: bin, conf, webapps, logs, lib, work — explain each.

items.q: How do you deploy a WAR file to Tomcat?
items.hint: Drop in webapps folder or use Manager app.

items.q: What is the server.xml file in Tomcat and what does it configure?
items.hint: Connectors, ports (8080/8443), virtual hosts, engine, realm.

items.q: How do you configure HTTPS/SSL in Tomcat?
items.hint: Connector in server.xml with keystoreFile and SSL attributes.

items.q: What is the difference between HTTP and AJP connectors in Tomcat?
items.hint: HTTP: direct browser traffic. AJP: from Apache web server proxy.

items.q: How do you tune JVM heap memory settings for Tomcat?
items.hint: CATALINA_OPTS: -Xms, -Xmx settings in setenv.sh/bat.

items.q: How do you troubleshoot a Tomcat application that is slow or unresponsive?
items.hint: Thread dumps, GC logs, heap analysis, catalina.out logs.

items.q: What are Tomcat access logs and how do you enable them?
items.hint: AccessLogValve in server.xml — logs every HTTP request.

items.q: How do you configure a JDBC connection pool in Tomcat?
items.hint: context.xml Resource element with DBCP pool settings.

items.q: What is the difference between Tomcat and IBM WebSphere?
items.hint: Tomcat: lightweight open-source. WebSphere: enterprise, heavyweight.

items.q: How do you manage multiple Tomcat instances on one server?
items.hint: Separate CATALINA_HOME, different ports per instance.

items.q: How do you restart Tomcat and check its status on Linux?
items.hint: ./shutdown.sh and ./startup.sh in bin/, or systemctl restart tomcat.

items.q: What are the main configuration files in Apache Tomcat and what does each do?
items.hint: server.xml (connectors/engine/host), web.xml (default servlets/filters), context.xml (datasources/resources), catalina.properties (classpath/security), logging.properties.

items.q: What would you do if Apache Tomcat is frequently going down?
items.hint: Check catalina.out for OOM/exceptions, review thread count (maxThreads), check heap settings (-Xmx), monitor GC, check for memory leaks in apps, consider JVM tuning.

items.q: What is horizontal vs vertical scaling in Apache Tomcat?
items.hint: Vertical: increase JVM heap, CPU/RAM on same server. Horizontal: add more Tomcat instances behind a load balancer (Apache httpd mod_jk or mod_proxy). Horizontal preferred for HA.

items.q: What solutions would you implement to avoid application crashes in Tomcat?
items.hint: JVM heap tuning, connection pool limits (maxTotal/maxIdle), thread pool sizing, watchdog/process monitor (systemd), graceful shutdown scripts, log rotation, health check endpoints.

items.q: What is Helm and why is it used in Kubernetes?
items.hint: Package manager for Kubernetes — templates YAML manifests.

items.q: What are Helm charts? Explain the chart directory structure.
items.hint: Chart.yaml, values.yaml, templates/ — explain each file.

items.q: What is the difference between helm install, upgrade, and rollback?
items.hint: install: first deploy, upgrade: update release, rollback: revert.

items.q: What are Helm values and how do you override them?
items.hint: values.yaml defaults, override with -f flag or --set.

items.q: What is a Helm release and how do you manage releases?
items.hint: helm list, helm history, helm status, helm uninstall.

items.q: What is the difference between Helm 2 and Helm 3?
items.hint: Helm 3 removed Tiller — direct cluster access, more secure.

items.q: How do you create a custom Helm chart from scratch?
items.hint: helm create my-chart — generates boilerplate structure.

items.q: What are Helm hooks and when do you use them?
items.hint: pre-install, post-upgrade — run jobs at lifecycle points.

items.q: How do you manage environment-specific configs with Helm?
items.hint: Separate values files: values-dev.yaml, values-prod.yaml.

items.q: How do you debug a failing Helm deployment?
items.hint: helm install --dry-run --debug, kubectl describe pod, events.

items.q: What is a Helm repository and how do you add one?
items.hint: helm repo add, helm repo update, helm search repo.

items.q: How do you handle Helm chart versioning?
items.hint: Chart.yaml version and appVersion fields, semantic versioning.

items.q: How do you use Helm with a Jenkins pipeline for deployments?
items.hint: helm upgrade --install in Jenkins stage, pass env-specific values file.

items.q: What is AWS IAM? Explain users, groups, roles, and policies.
items.hint: Identity and Access Management — principle of least privilege.

items.q: What is the difference between an IAM role and an IAM user?
items.hint: User: long-term credentials. Role: temporary, assumed by services.

items.q: What is an EC2 instance? How do you launch and connect to one?
items.hint: Virtual machine on AWS — launch via console/CLI, connect via SSH.

items.q: What are EC2 instance types and how do you choose the right one?
items.hint: t3 (general), c5 (compute), r5 (memory), m5 (balanced).

items.q: What is an EC2 Security Group and how does it work?
items.hint: Virtual firewall — inbound/outbound rules at instance level.

items.q: What is Amazon S3? How do you use it and what are bucket policies?
items.hint: Object storage — buckets, objects, versioning, lifecycle rules.

items.q: What is the difference between S3 Standard, S3 IA, and S3 Glacier?
items.hint: Frequency of access determines storage class — cost tradeoff.

items.q: What is Amazon EKS and how does it relate to Kubernetes?
items.hint: Managed Kubernetes control plane on AWS — you manage worker nodes.

items.q: How do you deploy an application to EKS?
items.hint: kubectl apply or Helm — same as Kubernetes but auth via aws eks.

items.q: What is the difference between EKS and ECS?
items.hint: EKS: Kubernetes-native. ECS: AWS-proprietary container service.

items.q: How do you set up IAM roles for EC2 instances to access S3?
items.hint: Instance profile — attach role, no hardcoded credentials needed.

items.q: What is a VPC in AWS and what are subnets, route tables, and NAT gateways?
items.hint: Virtual Private Cloud — network isolation for AWS resources.

items.q: How do you monitor AWS resources using CloudWatch?
items.hint: Metrics, logs, alarms, dashboards — set alerts on CPU, memory.

items.q: What is AWS Auto Scaling and when would you use it?
items.hint: Automatically add/remove EC2 instances based on demand.

items.q: How do you use AWS CLI to manage resources?
items.hint: aws ec2 describe-instances, aws s3 ls, aws iam list-users etc.

items.q: What is Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) and how does it compare to OpenShift?
items.hint: Managed Kubernetes on Azure — similar to EKS but Azure-native.

items.q: How do you create and manage an AKS cluster?
items.hint: az aks create, az aks get-credentials, kubectl commands.

items.q: How do you deploy an application to AKS using Helm?
items.hint: helm upgrade --install with kubeconfig pointing to AKS cluster.

items.q: What is Azure Redis Cache and what is it used for?
items.hint: Managed Redis service — caching, session storage, pub/sub.

items.q: How do you connect an application in AKS to Azure Redis?
items.hint: Connection string with access key, stored as Kubernetes Secret.

items.q: What is a Jumpbox (Bastion host) in Azure and why do you use it?
items.hint: Secure entry point to access private VMs — no public IP on target.

items.q: How do you connect to an Azure Jumpbox and access internal resources from it?
items.hint: SSH to jumpbox public IP → then SSH to internal VMs via private IP.

items.q: What is an Azure Virtual Network (VNet) and how does it relate to AKS?
items.hint: Private network for Azure resources — AKS nodes run inside VNet.

items.q: What is Azure Active Directory and how is it used with AKS?
items.hint: RBAC integration — AAD users/groups mapped to Kubernetes roles.

items.q: How do you monitor AKS workloads?
items.hint: Azure Monitor, Container Insights, Log Analytics workspace.

items.q: What is an Azure Resource Group and why is it important?
items.hint: Logical container for related Azure resources — manage together.

items.q: What is the difference between Azure Blob Storage and AWS S3?
items.hint: Both object storage — Azure uses containers inside storage accounts.

items.q: How do you scale AKS node pools manually and automatically?
items.hint: az aks scale, Cluster Autoscaler, HPA for pod-level scaling.

items.q: What is shell scripting and why is it useful in DevOps?
items.hint: Automate repetitive tasks, deployments, monitoring, backups.

items.q: What is the difference between sh, bash, and zsh?
items.hint: sh: POSIX standard. bash: most common Linux shell. zsh: extended.

items.q: How do you write a shell script? What is the shebang line?
items.hint: #!/bin/bash — tells OS which interpreter to use.

items.q: What are shell variables and how do you use them?
items.hint: NAME=value, $NAME to reference — no spaces around =.

items.q: How do you use if-else conditions in shell scripting?
items.hint: if [ condition ]; then ... elif ... else ... fi

items.q: How do you use for and while loops in shell scripting?
items.hint: for i in list; do ... done | while [ condition ]; do ... done

items.q: What are shell functions and how do you define them?
items.hint: function_name() { commands; } — reusable blocks of code.

items.q: How do you handle exit codes and error checking in shell scripts?
items.hint: $? for last exit code, set -e to exit on error, || for fallback.

items.q: What is the difference between single quotes and double quotes in bash?
items.hint: Single: no expansion. Double: variables and commands expanded.

items.q: How do you read user input and command-line arguments in a shell script?
items.hint: read for input, $1 $2 $@ for positional arguments.

items.q: How do you redirect output and handle stdin/stdout/stderr?
items.hint: >, >>, 2>, 2>&1, /dev/null — redirect or suppress.

items.q: What are some useful shell commands you use daily in DevOps?
items.hint: grep, awk, sed, cut, sort, tail, find, xargs, curl, ssh.

items.q: How do you schedule a shell script using cron?
items.hint: crontab -e — cron expression: minute hour day month weekday.

items.q: How do you write a script to check if a service is running and restart it?
items.hint: systemctl is-active, if not active then systemctl restart — run via cron.

items.q: How do you debug a shell script?
items.hint: bash -x script.sh — prints each command before execution.

items.q: What is Terraform and what problem does it solve?
items.hint: Infrastructure as Code tool — provision cloud resources using code instead of manual clicks.

items.q: What is the difference between Terraform and Ansible?
items.hint: Terraform: provisions infrastructure. Ansible: configures software on that infrastructure.

items.q: What are the main Terraform commands you use daily?
items.hint: terraform init, plan, apply, destroy, validate, fmt.

items.q: What is HCL and how is a basic .tf file structured?
items.hint: HashiCorp Configuration Language — terraform, provider, resource, variable, output blocks.

items.q: What is a Terraform provider and how do you configure AWS and Azure providers?
items.hint: Provider = plugin for a cloud. Declare in required_providers block, run terraform init.

items.q: What is the terraform.tfstate file and why is it important?
items.hint: State file = Terraform's memory of what it created. Never edit manually, store remotely for teams.

items.q: What is the difference between terraform plan and terraform apply?
items.hint: plan: dry run preview. apply: actually creates/changes/destroys real infrastructure.

items.q: What are Terraform variables and how do you use them?
items.hint: Input values defined in variables.tf, referenced with var.name, set via .tfvars or CLI.

items.q: What are Terraform outputs and when do you use them?
items.hint: Print values after apply (IP, URL) and pass values between modules.

items.q: What are Terraform modules and why do you use them?
items.hint: Reusable packages of .tf files — avoid repeating the same code across projects.

items.q: How do you store Terraform state remotely for a team?
items.hint: S3 backend for AWS, Azure Blob backend for Azure — enables state locking.

items.q: What are Terraform workspaces and how do you use them?
items.hint: Separate state per workspace — manage dev/uat/prod with same codebase.

items.q: How do you use Terraform to create an EC2 instance on AWS?
items.hint: aws_instance resource with ami, instance_type, security group, key_name.

items.q: How do you use Terraform to create an AKS cluster on Azure?
items.hint: azurerm_kubernetes_cluster resource with node pool, identity, dns_prefix.

items.q: How do you integrate Terraform with Jenkins CI/CD pipeline?
items.hint: terraform init/plan in pipeline, manual approval stage, then terraform apply.

items.q: What are Terraform best practices for a team environment?
items.hint: Remote state, pin provider versions, use modules, .gitignore tfstate, tag all resources.

items.q: What happens when you run terraform destroy?
items.hint: Deletes ALL resources tracked in state. Always run plan -destroy first to preview.

items.q: How do you import an existing cloud resource into Terraform?
items.hint: terraform import resource_type.name resource_id — adds to state without recreating.

items.q: What Linux distributions have you worked with?
items.hint: AIX, Red Hat Linux (RHEL), Ubuntu — production server experience.

items.q: What are the most important Linux commands for a DevOps engineer?
items.hint: ls, cd, cp, mv, rm, find, grep, tail, cat, chmod, chown, ps, top, df, du, netstat.

items.q: How do you check disk space and memory usage on Linux?
items.hint: df -h for disk, free -h for memory, top/htop for live usage.

items.q: How do you find and kill a process on Linux?
items.hint: ps aux | grep process, kill -9 PID, pkill process_name.

items.q: What is the difference between chmod and chown?
items.hint: chmod: change file permissions. chown: change file owner/group.

items.q: How do you check and manage running services on Linux?
items.hint: systemctl status/start/stop/restart service, journalctl -u service for logs.

items.q: How do you view and tail log files on Linux?
items.hint: tail -f /var/log/app.log, grep ERROR /var/log/app.log, less for large files.

items.q: What is crontab and how do you schedule jobs?
items.hint: crontab -e — minute hour day month weekday command format.

items.q: How do you check network connectivity and open ports on Linux?
items.hint: ping, curl, telnet, netstat -tuln, ss -tuln, nmap.

items.q: What is the difference between soft link and hard link?
items.hint: Soft link: pointer to file path. Hard link: pointer to inode — survives file rename.

items.q: How do you manage user accounts and groups on Linux?
items.hint: useradd, usermod, userdel, groupadd, passwd, /etc/passwd, /etc/group.

items.q: How do you check CPU usage and identify high CPU processes?
items.hint: top, htop, ps aux --sort=-%cpu, vmstat, mpstat.

items.q: What is SSH and how do you configure passwordless SSH?
items.hint: ssh-keygen to generate keys, copy public key to ~/.ssh/authorized_keys on target.

items.q: How do you set up and manage firewall rules on Linux?
items.hint: firewalld: firewall-cmd commands. iptables: iptables -A INPUT rules.

items.q: How do you troubleshoot a Linux server that is unreachable?
items.hint: Check ping, SSH port, firewall, sshd service, network interface, disk space, OOM killer.

items.q: What is the difference between /etc/profile, .bashrc, and .bash_profile?
items.hint: profile: login shell system-wide. bash_profile: login shell per-user. bashrc: non-login interactive shell.

items.q: How do you manage packages on Red Hat/RHEL Linux?
items.hint: yum install/remove/update, rpm -qa to list, yum list installed.

items.q: How do you check system logs on Linux?
items.hint: journalctl, /var/log/messages, /var/log/syslog, /var/log/secure for auth logs.

items.q: What is IBM WebSphere Application Server (WAS) and what is it used for?
items.hint: Enterprise Java application server — hosts Java EE applications, used in banking and enterprise.

items.q: What are the key components of WebSphere Application Server?
items.hint: Cell, Node, Node Agent, Deployment Manager (Dmgr), Application Server, Cluster.

items.q: What is the difference between WebSphere Base and WebSphere ND (Network Deployment)?
items.hint: Base: single server. ND: multiple servers with Deployment Manager for central management.

items.q: How do you start and stop WebSphere Application Server?
items.hint: startServer.sh / stopServer.sh in bin/ directory, or via Admin Console.

items.q: How do you access the WebSphere Administrative Console?
items.hint: https://hostname:9043/ibm/console — default admin port is 9043 for HTTPS.

items.q: How do you deploy an application (EAR/WAR) to WebSphere?
items.hint: Admin Console → Applications → Install, or wsadmin scripting tool.

items.q: What are JDBC Providers and Data Sources in WebSphere?
items.hint: JDBC Provider = database driver config. Data Source = connection pool to the database.

items.q: How do you configure JVM heap size in WebSphere?
items.hint: Admin Console → Server → JVM settings → Initial/Maximum heap size (-Xms/-Xmx).

items.q: What is WebSphere MQ and how does it integrate with WAS?
items.hint: IBM messaging middleware — JMS provider configured in WAS for async messaging.

items.q: How do you configure SSL in WebSphere?
items.hint: Key stores, trust stores, SSL configurations in Admin Console → Security → SSL.

items.q: How do you configure Virtual Hosts in WebSphere?
items.hint: Map application URLs to host aliases — Environment → Virtual Hosts in Admin Console.

items.q: How do you apply Fix Packs to WebSphere using Update Installer?
items.hint: Download fix pack, run updateSilent.sh with -installationDirectory and -installFix flags.

items.q: How do you troubleshoot application issues in WebSphere?
items.hint: Check SystemOut.log, SystemErr.log in logs/ directory, enable trace, check thread dumps.

items.q: What is thread pool configuration in WebSphere and why does it matter?
items.hint: Controls max concurrent threads per server — tune Web Container thread pool for high load.

items.q: How do you configure Global Security in WebSphere?
items.hint: Admin Console → Security → Global Security — enable application security, LDAP/AD integration.

items.q: What is wsadmin and how have you used it?
items.hint: Jython/Jacl scripting tool for automating WebSphere admin tasks — deploy apps, change config.

items.q: How do you set up WebSphere clustering?
items.hint: Create cluster in ND environment, add cluster members, use IHS (IBM HTTP Server) as load balancer.

items.q: What are the differences between WebSphere and Apache Tomcat?
items.hint: WebSphere: full Java EE, enterprise features, IBM support. Tomcat: lightweight, open-source, servlet only.

items.q: What is WebSphere DMGR (Deployment Manager) and what is its role?
items.hint: Central admin server in a WebSphere ND cell — manages node agents and application servers.

items.q: Will the application work if DMGR is down?
items.hint: Yes — DMGR is only needed for admin/config changes. App servers run independently once started.

items.q: Why is MQ used and why do we configure it with WebSphere?
items.hint: Async messaging — decouples apps, handles peak loads, ensures reliable delivery. WebSphere uses JMS/MQ for inter-app communication.

items.q: Why does memory leak occur in WebSphere and how do you fix it?
items.hint: ClassLoader leaks, thread locals not cleared, unclosed connections. Fix: heap dumps, GC tuning, connection pool config, app restart with root cause analysis.

items.q: What is Weblogic and how does it compare to WebSphere?
items.hint: Oracle's Java EE app server. Both are full JEE containers — WebLogic uses domains/managed servers, WebSphere uses cells/nodes/clusters.

items.q: How do you configure an SSL certificate in WebSphere without downtime?
items.hint: Use dynamic SSL config reload — add new cert to keystore, update SSL config in admin console, apply without restarting. Test on one node first in cluster.

items.q: What are SSL certificate use cases in enterprise middleware?
items.hint: HTTPS listeners, mutual TLS between app server and DB/MQ, client cert auth, internal service-to-service encryption.

items.q: Describe 2 major real-time production incidents you have handled.
items.hint: Structure: incident description → impact → root cause → immediate fix → long-term prevention. Use your WebSphere OOM or Tomcat thread exhaustion examples.

items.q: What is Kubernetes and what problems does it solve?
Explain the difference between a Pod, Deployment, and Service in Kubernetes.
items.hint: You have created and managed all three in your projects.

items.q: What is the role of a ReplicaSet in Kubernetes?
How do you expose an application running in Kubernetes to the outside world?
items.hint: Services: ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer — and Routes in OpenShift.

items.q: What is a Kubernetes Namespace and why is it used?
items.hint: Logical isolation of resources — used for dev/uat/prod separation.

items.q: How do you perform a rolling update and rollback in Kubernetes?
items.hint: kubectl rollout commands, Helm rollback.

items.q: What are ConfigMaps and Secrets? How are they different?
What is a PersistentVolume and PersistentVolumeClaim?
How do you scale a deployment manually and automatically in Kubernetes?
items.hint: kubectl scale, HorizontalPodAutoscaler (HPA).

items.q: What is a DaemonSet and when would you use it?
How do you troubleshoot a Pod stuck in CrashLoopBackOff?
items.hint: kubectl logs, kubectl describe pod — practical steps.

items.q: How do you manage CPU and memory resource limits for Pods?
items.hint: Requests vs limits defined in the YAML spec.

items.q: What is the difference between OpenShift and plain Kubernetes?
items.hint: OpenShift adds security, Routes, Projects, image registry on top of Kubernetes.

items.q: What is a Route in OpenShift and how does it differ from a Kubernetes Ingress?
items.hint: HAProxy router built-in — just oc expose svc to create a route.

items.q: How do you create a Project in OpenShift and manage access to it?
items.hint: oc new-project, oc adm policy add-role-to-user.

items.q: What is the difference between a DeploymentConfig and a Deployment in OpenShift?
items.hint: DC: OpenShift-specific with triggers. Deployment: standard Kubernetes — preferred now.

items.q: How do you configure networking for applications in OpenShift?
items.hint: SDN, Routes, Services — expose, configure TLS termination.

items.q: What is the OpenShift internal image registry and how do you push images to it?
items.hint: docker login to registry URL, tag image, docker push.

items.q: How does OpenShift handle Security Context Constraints (SCCs)?
items.hint: SCCs are stricter than Kubernetes by default — containers cannot run as root.

items.q: What are OpenShift Operators and how have you used them?
items.hint: OperatorHub, managing stateful applications lifecycle.

items.q: How do you monitor applications running on OpenShift?
items.hint: Splunk integration and OpenShift built-in monitoring stack.

items.q: What is the oc command-line tool and how does it differ from kubectl?
items.hint: oc is a superset of kubectl with OpenShift-specific commands.

items.q: How do you configure authentication and authorization in OpenShift?
items.hint: OAuth, LDAP/AD integration, RBAC — role bindings at project level.

items.q: Walk me through setting up a complete CI/CD pipeline deploying to OpenShift using Jenkins.
items.hint: Bitbucket webhook → Jenkins build → Docker image → Helm deploy to OpenShift.

items.q: What is Docker and how is it different from a virtual machine?
What is a Dockerfile? Walk me through the key instructions you use.
items.hint: FROM, RUN, COPY, CMD, EXPOSE, ENV — key Dockerfile instructions.

items.q: What is the difference between a Docker image and a Docker container?
How do you build a Docker image and push it to a container registry?
items.hint: docker build, docker tag, docker push.

items.q: What container registries have you worked with apart from Docker Hub?
items.hint: OpenShift internal registry, AWS ECR, Azure ACR.

items.q: How do you manage and monitor running containers?
items.hint: docker ps, docker logs, docker stats.

items.q: What is a multi-stage Docker build and why would you use it?
items.hint: Reduces final image size by separating build and runtime stages.

items.q: How do Docker volumes work and when do you use them?
items.hint: Persistent storage that survives container restarts.

items.q: What is Docker Compose and how does it differ from Kubernetes?
items.hint: Local multi-container orchestration vs production-grade platform.

items.q: How do you optimize a Docker image for size and security?
items.hint: Minimal base images, .dockerignore file, running as non-root user.

items.q: Your Jenkins pipeline is failing at the deployment stage to OpenShift. How do you troubleshoot it?
items.hint: Check Jenkins console logs, oc get events, pod describe, image pull errors.

items.q: A Pod is running but the application is not accessible from outside. What steps do you take?
items.hint: Check Service selector, Route configuration, pod logs, network policies.

items.q: You need to deploy a new version of an application with zero downtime. How do you approach it?
items.hint: Rolling update strategy, readiness probes, Helm upgrade.

items.q: Your Helm chart deployment failed halfway through. How do you recover?
items.hint: helm rollback, check release history, debug with --dry-run flag.

items.q: A client reports slow application performance in production. What is your approach?
items.hint: Splunk logs, pod resource usage, HPA scaling, node capacity checks.

items.q: How would you set up a new OpenShift environment for a banking application from scratch?
items.hint: Projects, networking, image registry, CI/CD pipeline, secrets, monitoring.

items.q: Your team wants to migrate from manual deployments to a fully automated CI/CD pipeline. How do you plan it?
items.hint: Bitbucket → Jenkins → Helm → OpenShift — walk through the design.

items.q: Describe a Severity-1 production incident you handled. How did you resolve it?
items.hint: Explain the troubleshooting approach: logs, root cause, fix, RCA document.

items.q: An IIS application is throwing 503 errors in production. How do you diagnose and fix it?
items.hint: Application pool stopped, worker process crash — Event Viewer, recycle pool.

items.q: Your Ansible playbook is failing on 3 out of 10 servers. How do you handle it?
items.hint: --limit to target failed hosts, check facts, idempotency issues.

items.q: An EKS pod is not pulling an image from ECR. What do you do?
items.hint: IAM role for EC2 node group, ECR policy, docker login to ECR.

items.q: Your Azure Redis cache is causing high latency in production. How do you troubleshoot?
items.hint: Connection pool exhaustion, eviction policy, cache hit ratio, scaling tier.

items.q: A WebSphere server is unresponsive and not serving requests. What do you do?
items.hint: Check SystemOut.log, thread dump, JVM heap, restart server, check application logs.

items.q: You need to create AWS infrastructure for a new project using Terraform. How do you start?
items.hint: Write provider, VPC, subnets, EC2/EKS resources, remote state in S3, run init/plan/apply.

items.q: A Linux server has very high CPU usage. How do you identify and fix the issue?
items.hint: top/htop to find process, check logs, kill if rogue, tune app config or scale up.
